mission.title: strimzi: Support for AWS MSK IAM authorization
mission.description: Support for AWS MSK IAM authorization. This issue affects 32+ users.

steps.title: Identify strimzi feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your strimzi deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=strimzi
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Support for AWS MSK IAM authorization

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant strimzi resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Hi! We are looking at using Strimzi to deploy Kafka Connect clusters using Kafka brokers deployed using Amazon MSK. We'd like to use MSK's IAM feature for access control within the Kafka cluster (http

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for AWS MSK IAM authorization
steps.description:
Update the configuration generator scripts to support arbitrary configuration properties. The `KAFKA_CONNECT_ADDITIONAL_CONFIG` environment variable is open ended and non-opinionated. For this reason, it can be used to pass in additional configuration properties. This allows for support of arbitrary
```yaml
ssl.truststore.location=<PATH_TO_TRUST_STORE_FILE>
security.protocol=SASL_SSL
sasl.mechanism=AWS_MSK_IAM
sasl.jaas.config=software.amazon.msk.auth.iam.IAMLoginModule required;
sasl.client.callback.handler.class=software.amazon.msk.auth.iam.IAMClientCallbackHandler
```

steps.title: Confirm Support for AWS MSK IAM authorization is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The root cause is: Update the configuration generator scripts to support arbitrary configuration properties. The `KAFKA_CONNECT_ADDITIONAL_CONFIG` environment variable is open ended and non-opinionated. For this reason, it can be used to pass in additional configuration properties. This allows for support of arbitrary authentications mechanisms that were not possible before.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with strimzi installed or the issue environment reproducible.
